# Joints Constraints

> 3D joint constraints connecting rigid bodies

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** integration · godot

## What this does

3D joint constraints connecting rigid bodies

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## What must be true

- **implementation:** Feature implemented in C++ engine core
- **platform_agnostic:** Works across desktop, web, mobile platforms

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Hinge Joint** — when Revolute joint (door hinge) is requested, then Revolute joint (door hinge) completed.
- **Slider Joint** — when Prismatic joint (piston) is requested, then Prismatic joint (piston) completed.
- **Ball Joint** — when Spherical joint (shoulder) is requested, then Spherical joint (shoulder) completed.
- **Cone Twist Joint** — when Twist with cone limit is requested, then Twist with cone limit completed.
- **6 Dof Joint** — when Full 6-axis freedom with limits is requested, then Full 6-axis freedom with limits completed.
